data.reasoningText:
This is very revealing. Common.csproj is being evaluated 52 times! That's way too many. The redundant project references are causing excessive evaluations.

Let me also check for the solution configurations - it has 6 platform configs (Debug|Any CPU, Debug|x64, Debug|x86, Release|Any CPU, Release|x64, Release|x86) but all projects map to "Any CPU". The extra x64/x86 platforms are unnecessary bloat.

Let me also verify: with `dotnet build -m` without `--no-restore`, restore runs every single build. That's the 3s RestoreTask showing up every time.
 
I'm also noticing that TargetFramework is duplicated across the csproj files even though it's already defined in Directory.Build.props, and there are transitive project references that could be removed — BusinessLogic doesn't need to directly reference Common since DataAccess already brings it in, and WebApi is pulling in redundant dependencies through BusinessLogic.

The real performance killer is that Common.csproj gets evaluated 52 times due to these overlapping references. Beyond that, XML documentation generation and code style analysis are running on every build when they should only happen in CI, and the restore step is adding unnecessary overhead since no `--no-restore` flag is being used.

Now I'm going to work through the concrete fixes for each of these issues.
